flashcards.front: What is bus organization in computer architecture?
flashcards.back: Bus organization refers to the way in which different components of a computer system, such as the CPU, memory, and input/output devices, communicate with each other.

flashcards.front: What is the primary function of a bus in a computer system?
flashcards.back: The primary function of a bus is to transmit data between different components of the system.

flashcards.front: What are the different types of bus organizations?
flashcards.back: The different types of bus organizations include serial bus, parallel bus, and hybrid bus.
A serial bus is a type of bus organization where data is transmitted one bit at a time over a single communication channel.
A parallel bus is a type of bus organization where data is transmitted multiple bits at a time over multiple communication channels.
A hybrid bus is a type of bus organization that combines the features of serial and parallel buses.
Bus width refers to the number of bits that can be transmitted over a bus at a time.
Bus speed refers to the rate at which data is transmitted over a bus, typically measured in megahertz (MHz) or gigahertz (GHz).
Bus arbitration is the process of resolving conflicts when multiple devices try to access the bus at the same time.
Bus priority refers to the order in which devices are granted access to the bus when multiple devices request access at the same time.
Bus protocol refers to the set of rules that govern the communication between devices over a bus.
Bus bandwidth refers to the maximum amount of data that can be transmitted over a bus in a given amount of time.
